word: H3239 - יָנֹוחַ - Yânôwach - yaw-no'-akh or (with enclitic) Yanowchah yaw-no'-khaw - Janoah

sections.paragraph:
Original: ינוחה ינוח

Transliteration: yânôach yânôchâh

Phonetic: yaw-no'-akh

BDB Definition:

Janoah or Janohah = "He rests"
  1. a place apparently in the north of Galilee in Naphtali, taken by Tiglath-pileser in his first incursion into Palestine

Origin: from H3240

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
